In this paper, we extract information from the transient surface vibrations simultaneously measured at a sparse set of object points using the dual-shutter camera.
We model the geometry of an elastic wave generated at the moment an object’s surface is disturbed and use the model to localize the disturbance source for various materials.
We also show that transient object vibrations contain additional cues about the impact force and the impacting object’s material properties.
